title: SSc-MIM: Skin Fibrosis Molecular Interaction Map in Diffuse Cutaneous Systemic Sclerosis
description: First module of a curated, SBGN-compliant Molecular Interaction Map (MIM) for systemic sclerosis (SSc), built in CellDesigner and paired with a single-cell transcriptomic overlay from SSc skin biopsies (Tabib 2021, GSE138669). Four sub-modules cover IFN-I signalling, TGF-beta / fibroblast-to-myofibroblast transition, endothelial-to-mesenchymal transition and vasculopathy, and IL-6 / IL-4 / IL-13 / B-cell crosstalk. Contains 385 species and 175 reactions across 17 compartments, with 158/159 reactions PubMed-cited (355 unique PMIDs) and MI2CAST-annotated. Map content is licensed under CC-BY 4.0; analysis code under MIT.
references: Mazein A, Ostaszewski M, Kuperstein I, et al. Systems medicine disease maps: community-driven comprehensive representation of disease mechanisms. npj Syst Biol Appl 2018;4:21. PMID 30564456.
Mazein A, et al. A guide for developing comprehensive systems biology maps of disease mechanisms. Front Bioinform 2023. PMID 38064118.
Tabib T, et al. SFRP2/DPP4 and FMO1/LSP1 define major fibroblast populations in human skin (and SSc). Nat Commun 2021.
notes: Map content (curation/, docs/, figures/) is released under CC-BY 4.0. Analysis code (scripts/, analysis/, .github/) is released under the MIT License. See LICENSE for full terms. The CellDesigner XML is SBML L2v4 with CellDesigner annotations; it opens in CellDesigner v4.4+ and any MINERVA Platform instance.
